regime_justification: USDJPY spiked to 160.73 intraday before sharp reversal on MOF 'final warning' intervention threat; yen seeing biggest daily gain since mid-March; BOJ 6-3 split shows hawkish dissent brewing but no near-term hike signal; cross-asset unwind signatures present

domino_stage.label: Shock / Trigger
domino_stage.justification: MOF Vice Minister Mimura issued 'final warning' after USDJPY broke 160.73, triggering sharp 400+ pip yen rally to 156.5; intervention verbally fired; carry liquidating intraday

1w.label: Likely
1w.driver: MOF intervention threat credible after 'final warning' language; USDJPY rejected 160.7 violently; but Ueda gave no June hike clarity, limiting yen support

2w.label: Likely
2w.driver: BOJ 6-3 split signals June hike live but not locked; oil at $114 keeps imported inflation pressure high; if USDJPY re-tests 160 MOF will act

3w.label: Watchful
3w.driver: Scenario-weighted: either MOF intervenes and carry unwinds further OR market fades verbal threat and spread/vol dynamics stabilize; June BOJ meeting key catalyst

4w.label: Watchful
4w.driver: June BOJ decision (June 15-16) determines medium-term path; if hike delivered spread narrows 25bp and unwind extends; if hold carry rebuilds but fragile

bottom_line: Trigger fired today: USDJPY broke 160.7, MOF issued 'final warning,' pair dumped 400 pips to 156.5 in sharpest yen rally since March. Stage 3 Shock underway. BOJ 6-3 split shows three members wanted 1.0% hike immediately, but Ueda punted citing Iran uncertainty—keeping real rates deeply negative and limiting yen support. Oil at $114, JGB 10Y spiking to 2.53%, spread still 187bp, VIX grinding lower, MOVE calm at 74—cross-asset contagion limited so far but today's intraday violence confirms carry fragility. Verbal intervention bought time; actual intervention or June hike needed to sustain yen strength. Grades: B/B/C/C—unwind early-stage but policy gap unresolved.

narrative.p1: Stage 3 Shock fired today. USDJPY spiked to 160.73 in early London session, triggering MOF Vice Minister Mimura's 'final warning'—the strongest verbal intervention language since September 2022. The pair dumped 400 pips to 156.57, the sharpest single-day yen rally since mid-March, as carry shorts covered. Finance Minister Katayama confirmed direct talks with US Treasury Secretary Bessent and told G7 she's watching FX with 'high sense of urgency,' signaling coordinated intervention is on the table. Yesterday's BOJ decision held rates at 0.75% but the 6-3 vote split—three members wanted an immediate hike to 1.0%—was the most divided outcome since Ueda took office. Governor Ueda punted, citing 'lingering uncertainty' from the Iran war, and gave no clear signal for June, disappointing yen bulls. Oil pulled back from a $126 Brent intraday spike to $114 after reports Trump is weighing expanded military action in Iran. The blockade holds, Hormuz remains shut, and imported inflation continues to burn. Regime flipped red from yesterday's yellow as the intraday violence confirms carry fragility despite limited cross-asset contagion so far.
narrative.p2: Quant anchors: US-JP 10Y spread = 189bp (4.42 - 2.53), computed spread near cycle wide but both yields rising together. JGB 10Y at 2.531% broke prior 52-week high as BOJ's 3-dissent vote signals hawkish pressure building despite the hold. Carry-to-vol proxy: 189bp / ~7.7% (1M implied, stale data but directionally low single-digit) ≈ 24.5x, still elevated but vol bid missing. USDJPY 5-day pace: -1.8% (computed from 160.66 high on 4/29 to 156.57 close today); MOF historically reacts to pace > 2%/week, we're at threshold. ¥/bbl crude = 17,854 yen (114 × 156.6), off the 19,700 intraday peak but still cycle high; BOJ raised FY2026 CPI forecast to 2.8% (from 1.9%) and cut growth to 0.5%, classic stagflation setup. USDJPY distance from 60d mean: likely stretched to downside now after 400-pip dump, but no data to confirm. We are now at Domino Stage 3 (Shock/Trigger): MOF verbally intervened with 'final warning' language, triggering forced carry liquidation intraday. Grades: 1w=B, 2w=B (data-driven): the 6-3 BOJ split keeps June hike live (~80% priced per prior commentary) but Ueda's refusal to pre-signal means the market must price two-way risk. If USDJPY re-tests 160, MOF will likely execute spot intervention. But if yen fades today's rally (Ueda gave no policy support), carry can rebuild on the still-wide 189bp spread. Oil at $114 and rising JGB yields keep pressure on, but VIX at 18.8 and MOVE at 74 show equity/rates complacency intact—cross-asset cascade has not triggered. Applied unwind prior to 2w grade: would otherwise grade C given Ueda's dovish tone, but the 6-3 split + MOF credibility + oil shock tilt it to B. Data is ambiguous (verbal threat vs. no policy follow-through), prior leans one notch more alert.
narrative.p3: BTC at $76,316, down 1.1% and printing lowest open in over a week. Crypto is draining as risk-off rotation accelerates on Iran escalation fears and Fed's higher-for-longer signal (3-member dissent). ETF inflows slowed sharply in late April after $2B month-to-date; exchange reserves at multi-year lows but that's a supply story, not demand. BTC correlation to DXY remains negative (DXY -0.6% today as yen spiked), and correlation to equities positive but weaker than 2025—BTC is trading as a leveraged risk asset, not a macro hedge. The $78.5k resistance Ueda cited held; failure to reclaim signals range-bound $75-78k near-term. For crypto positioning: today's FX violence is a warning shot. If carry unwinds further (MOF intervenes or BOJ hikes June), expect BTC to test $70k as leveraged longs deleverage. If verbal threat fades and USDJPY grinds back to 160, BTC can retest $80k on resumed risk-on flows. The Iran war overhang (oil spiking, growth slowing, inflation rising) is a headwind to all duration/leverage, including crypto. Stay hedged; the fragility is real.

horizon_rationale.w1_w2: Week 1 (B): MOF 'final warning' is credible given Mimura's language and Katayama-Bessent coordination; USDJPY rejected 160.7 violently. But Ueda gave no June hike signal, so yen rally depends on verbal threat sustaining fear. If market fades and USDJPY drifts back to 159, MOF must act or credibility erodes. Week 2 (B): BOJ 6-3 split keeps June hike (~June 15-16 meeting) live; if oil holds $110+ and core CPI prints firm in May, Ueda may pre-signal. But baseline is cautious hold, so unwind depends on MOF follow-through. Both weeks carry high two-way risk; grades reflect elevated probability of carry liquidation but not certainty.
horizon_rationale.w2_w3: Week 2 (B) to Week 3 (C): scenario-weighted shift. By week 3, either (a) MOF has intervened and/or BOJ has signaled June hike clearly, in which case unwind extends and grade would be A-B, OR (b) verbal threat fades, USDJPY stabilizes 155-158, and carry rebuilds on 189bp spread, in which case grade drops to D. The C grade reflects this divergence: no single path dominates. Key catalysts in this window: any actual MOF spot intervention, May Tokyo CPI (released early May), and any Ueda/BOJ member speeches. The Iran war remains the wildcard—if Hormuz reopens, oil crashes, inflation fears ease, and yen weakens; if Trump strikes Iran, oil spikes to $130+, stagflation fears explode, and forced unwind accelerates.
horizon_rationale.w3_w4: Week 3 (C) to Week 4 (C): both scenario-weighted. By week 4 we likely have clarity on June BOJ decision (meeting June 15-16, so just outside 4w window but expectations will be set). If June hike is locked in, spread narrows 25bp to ~165bp, USDJPY trades 150-153, and unwind risk remains elevated (grade B-C). If BOJ holds again citing Iran uncertainty, carry rebuilds on wide spread but remains fragile on intervention risk (grade C-D). The C/C reflects balanced risk: the policy gap is the dominant medium-term driver, and it remains unresolved. Oil trajectory and US growth data (Q1 GDP, May payrolls) will also matter for Fed-BOJ divergence. Non-monotonic structure (B/B/C/C) reflects near-term trigger risk (MOF action, BOJ surprise) fading into scenario-weighted uncertainty by weeks 3-4.

watch_list: MOF actual spot intervention (not just verbal)—if USDJPY re-tests 159.5-160 in next 48 hours, execution likely
BOJ Governor Ueda or board member speeches signaling June hike preparation—market only 80% priced, clear pre-signal would compress spread fast
May Tokyo CPI (released early May, ~1 week out)—forecast 1.8-1.9% YoY core; upside surprise would force BOJ's hand for June
Trump Iran military action decision—Axios reported briefing on expanded strikes; execution would spike oil to $120-130 and force global policy response
USDJPY 1M risk reversals flipping to JPY-call premium—not retrieved today but prior briefs flagged this; would confirm options market pricing unwind
